reasoning: A has the most genuinely distinct set: Slosh (a fluid blob as the ball, surface tension as the rules), Candlewake (the sport is tending a zero-g flame, no body or ball at all), and Spinwright (angular momentum and matching tumble) each live in a different conceptual space, and Candlewake especially escapes the body-ball-trajectory frame entirely. C is close and beautifully crafted, with Centrifuge (restoring fake gravity via spin and the Coriolis slant) and Passant (balance as a resource teammates literally pass each other) standing out, but its Tetherball and Long Arc overlap A's tether and straight-line-puck ideas. B is the weakest: its five are sketchy one-liners and several cluster around the same body-pushing/contact-and-mass motif (No-Floor Siege, Collapse Choir, Mass Court), with thin development and little to build on.
